curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: Someone on my team once described our communications strategy as 'controlled chaos.' I said — which part is controlled? Nobody laughed. That told me a lot.
quotes.speaker: Michael Bloomberg
quotes.context: Remarks at the U.S. Conference of Mayors Winter Meeting, Washington D.C., January 2020
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bloomberg's humor is dry, managerial and slightly impatient, and this kind of boardroom-inflected self-analysis fits his actual speaking style. The tell is that the 'nobody laughed' beat is a comedy technique slightly too deliberate for Bloomberg's typically clipped delivery.

quotes.text: There's a version of tax policy that I genuinely find fascinating, and then there's the version I have to explain at a town hall at seven in the morning, and those are very different conversations.
quotes.speaker: Paul Ryan
quotes.context: Remarks during a Q&A session at the Ripon Society annual dinner, Washington D.C., February 2017
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Ryan was famous for his earnest budget-wonk enthusiasm and this captures that slight weariness between his intellectual passion and political reality. The tell is that Ryan would almost certainly have named a specific provision rather than just saying 'tax policy' generally.

quotes.text: The Internet is not something that you just dump something on. It's not a big truck. It's a series of tubes.
quotes.speaker: Ted Stevens
quotes.context: Republican U.S. Senator from Alaska, then chairman of the Senate Commerce Committee, attempting to describe how the internet works during a June 28, 2006 committee debate on net neutrality.

quotes.text: I admit it -- last year was rough.
quotes.speaker: Barack Obama
quotes.context: At the 2014 White House Correspondents' Dinner, Obama opened his self-deprecating riff by candidly conceding his difficult 2013.

source.title: Remarks by the President at White House Correspondents' Dinner (May 3, 2014) | whitehouse.gov archives

quotes.text: I will not make age an issue of this campaign. I am not going to exploit, for political purposes, my opponent's youth and inexperience.
quotes.speaker: Ronald Reagan
quotes.context: His famous comeback at the 1984 debate against Walter Mondale

source.title: CPD: October 21, 1984 Debate Transcript | Commission on Presidential Debates

quotes.text: People keep asking me if I miss being in the Senate. And I tell them — yes, genuinely, I do. Then I remember the parking situation and I feel much better.
quotes.speaker: Al Franken
quotes.context: Interview with Marc Maron on WTF with Marc Maron, podcast episode released June 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Franken's comedy background means he naturally structures observations as little setups and payoffs, and griping about a mundane logistical annoyance is exactly his register. The giveaway is that real Franken tends to go slightly more specific and self-lacerating before landing.
